summary: Every system must be able to answer, for any operational datum, WHO changed it, WHAT changed (old→new), and WHEN — with the complete history via a generic audit-trail, not per-table columns.

rules.rule: Provide a generic change-capture audit-trail recording every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on audited tables as {table, op, row id, old, new, who, when} — uniform, field-level, complete history, in one place, not re-implemented per table.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Build the audit-trail out of per-table audit columns (updated_by / updated_at) or mistake row-lifecycle timestamps for it — they store only the last touch and lose field-level before/after and the full history.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Record as the actor the real authenticated identity behind the change — a privileged/automated path carries (or impersonates under governance) a real attributable identity; anonymous or shared-service writes to audited tables defeat the canon.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Expose the audit-trail to client/tenant-facing surfaces — it is governance/admin-only, and read access is an explicit, privileged, governed path.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Run the generic old/new-capturing trigger on a secret store or sensitive-value table — it would replicate the secret inside the audit log; such tables are excluded or captured metadata-only (who/when/table/op, no values).

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Partition the trail, keep a hot window bound to the legal retention requirement, and archive past partitions to cold storage — true deletion of trail rows happens only when a legal obligation requires it, as a governed, logged exception, never routine cleanup.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
